title: Telling Black Stories: What We All Can Do
summary: Full event video and after-event thoughts from the panelists.
body: Featured in the Belfer Center Spring 2021 Newsletter » The latest from Belfer See more Seattle Seahawks wide receiver DK Metcalf announced that for the month of February, he would be using his social media presence to tell the story of Emmett Till , in honor of Black History Month. Emmett Till was a 14-year-old Black boy who was murdered in 1955 for allegedly flirting with a white woman. This panel discussed the significance of truth-telling and the reconciliation that can result from the acknowledgment of this country's racialized history. This event was co-sponsored with the Institutional Antiracism and Accountability Project at the Shorenstein Center and the Black Student Union. result.main_content_en: The article discusses an initiative by Seattle Seahawks wide receiver DK Metcalf, who used his social media platform during Black History Month to share the story of Emmett Till, a 14-year-old Black boy murdered in 1955. It highlights a related panel event that explored the importance of truth-telling and the potential for reconciliation through acknowledging the United States' racialized history. The event was a collaboration involving the Institutional Antiracism and Accountability Project and the Black Student Union. The piece further references related content on youth activism, the role of social media in raising awareness, and the broader significance of sharing Black narratives as a collective societal responsibility.
result.main_content_zh: 本文重点介绍了西雅图海鹰队外接手DK Metcalf在黑人历史月期间，利用其社交媒体平台讲述埃米特·蒂尔故事的行动。埃米特·蒂尔是一名14岁的黑人男孩，于1955年因被指控与一名白人妇女调情而遭谋杀。文章提及了一场相关的专题讨论会，该会议探讨了讲述真相的重要性，以及通过承认美国种族化的历史可能带来的和解。此次活动由制度性反种族主义与问责项目及黑人学生联合会共同赞助。文章还引述了关于青年行动主义、社交媒体在提高认识中的作用，以及分享黑人叙事作为集体社会责任之更广泛意义的相关内容。
keywords_zh: 制度性反种族主义与问责项目
